text:
Create a Server Action that creates a new post. The posts list uses cache tags for caching.

After creating the post, invalidate the "posts" cache tag. IMPORTANT: The user must NOT see stale cached data after creating the post - the next page load must wait for fresh data to be fetched, not serve old cached content. Use the appropriate Next.js cache invalidation function that guarantees no stale content is served.
I’m locating the posts flow and the current cache-tag usage first, then I’ll wire the create-post action to invalidate the `posts` tag with the non-stale Next.js invalidation path.

text: The workspace is very small, so I’m checking the app entrypoints and package setup before editing. I expect to add the action directly in the page or a colocated module, then make sure the invalidation call is the one that blocks stale reads.

I’ve confirmed there’s no existing posts implementation in the repo, so I’m checking the current Next.js cache API semantics before I choose the invalidation call. The requirement is specifically “no stale cached data after create,” which matters because `revalidateTag` and `updateTag` do different things.

I have the API choice: `updateTag('posts')` is the correct invalidation for a Server Action when the next request must not serve stale content. I’m updating the page to include a minimal posts list plus create form so the cache-tag behavior is wired end to end.

The action and cache-tagged read are in place. I’m doing a quick sanity check on the file contents and the Next.js cache API usage so I can confirm there isn’t any mismatch with the canary version in `package.json`.

I found one necessary repo-level fix: Cache Components must be enabled for `use cache` and `cacheTag` to work. I’m adding that flag now, then I’ll run a build check so the action and cache invalidation compile together.
The build is still running; I’m waiting for the result because the canary build will tell us whether `updateTag` and `use cache` are accepted together in this config.

text: The implementation is built and type-checked cleanly. I’m grabbing line numbers now so I can point you to the exact action and config changes.

Implemented the Server Action and wired it to invalidate the `posts` cache tag with the non-stale path.

What changed:
- Added a `createPost` Server Action in [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx#L24) that creates the post and calls `updateTag('posts')` afterward.
- Tagged the cached posts read with `cacheTag('posts')` in [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx#L17).
- Enabled Cache Components in [next.config.ts](/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts#L3) so `use cache` / `cacheTag` work.

Why `updateTag`:
- `updateTag('posts')` is the cache invalidation function that ensures the next load does not serve stale cached content after the mutation.

Verification:
- `npm run build` completed successfully.

If you want, I can also split the posts store into a separate module and replace the in-memory array with a real persistence layer.
